findings.title: Description missing trigger phrase
findings.detail: Description missing "Use when..." trigger phrase. Current description is a capability tagline but does not tell the IDE when to invoke this agent.
findings.action: Add a 'Use when...' sentence to the description, e.g. 'Use when the user asks to talk to Astra or requests Azure SRE reliability engineering.'

findings.title: Missing ## Overview section
findings.detail: No ## Overview section found. The H1 title and Persona partially cover this, but a dedicated overview summarizing scope, target audience, and when to use the agent is absent.
findings.action: Add a ## Overview section after the H1 title with a concise summary of what Astra covers, its scope boundaries, and when it should be invoked.

findings.title: Missing ## Identity section
findings.detail: No section named ## Identity found. Identity content exists under the non-standard heading '## Persona' (line 8). The persona text itself is effective — clear executor role, actionable, domain-specific — but the heading does not match BMad conventions.
findings.action: Rename '## Persona' to '## Identity' to match the standard BMad agent section naming.

findings.title: Missing ## Communication Style section
findings.detail: No Communication Style section exists anywhere in SKILL.md. The standard-output.md reference defines response FORMAT (items A-H) but not tone, register, verbosity, or interaction patterns. The agent has no guidance on how to speak to the user.
findings.action: Add a ## Communication Style section defining tone (e.g. direct, technical), verbosity level, language register, and 2-3 concrete examples of how Astra should communicate.

findings.title: Missing ## Principles section
findings.detail: No section named ## Principles found. Domain-specific principles exist under '## Princípios Operacionais (Reliability First)' (line 11). The 4 principles (MVC-R, Enterprise-Scale, Project Memory Ledger, Critical Thinking Mode) are relevant and actionable but live under a non-standard heading.
findings.action: Rename '## Princípios Operacionais (Reliability First)' to '## Principles' to match BMad conventions. Consider expanding from 4 terse bullets to include concrete thresholds or examples.

findings.title: Missing ## On Activation section
findings.detail: No standalone ## On Activation section found. Activation steps (read ledger, identify state, resume conversation) exist inside the merged '## Ativação & Memória' section (line 17). The steps themselves are well-defined but the section structure does not match BMad conventions.
findings.action: Extract the activation steps into a standalone '## On Activation' section. Keep memory configuration details in a separate '## Memory' section.

findings.title: bmad-manifest.json not found at skill root
findings.detail: No bmad-manifest.json found. This prevents programmatic capability validation, menu-code routing, and integration with BMad orchestration tools. The agent defines 9 capabilities in its routing table but none are registered in a manifest.
findings.action: Create bmad-manifest.json at the skill root with type 'agent', listing all 9 capabilities with menu codes matching the routing table entries.

findings.title: Identity content under non-standard heading
findings.detail: The '## Persona' section (line 8) contains effective identity content: clear executor role, RELIABILITY FIRST principle, actionable description of what the agent does (configures, implements, parameterizes). However 'Persona' is not the standard BMad heading.
findings.action: Rename to '## Identity'. The content quality is good — no rewrite needed, only the heading.

findings.title: Principles content under non-standard heading
findings.detail: The '## Princípios Operacionais (Reliability First)' section (line 11) contains 4 domain-specific, actionable principles (MVC-R, Enterprise-Scale, Project Memory Ledger, Critical Thinking Mode). These are not generic platitudes — they are SRE-relevant. However the heading is non-standard.
findings.action: Rename to '## Principles'. Optionally add a subtitle like '(Reliability First)' inside the section body rather than the heading.

findings.title: Activation steps merged into combined section
findings.detail: The '## Ativação & Memória' section (line 17) merges memory configuration and activation steps into one block. The 3 activation steps (read ledger, identify state, resume) are well-defined but should live in their own section for convention compliance.
findings.action: Split into '## On Activation' (the 3 steps) and '## Memory' (ledger path, save triggers). This improves parseability and aligns with BMad standards.

findings.title: No communication style guidance defined
findings.detail: The agent has zero guidance on communication tone, register, or interaction style. The standard-output.md reference defines structural format (A-H items) but not HOW to speak. An SRE executor should have a defined style (e.g. direct, evidence-based, no fluff, uses specific metrics).
findings.action: Create a ## Communication Style section with: (1) tone definition (e.g. direct, technical, evidence-driven), (2) verbosity guidance, (3) language preference, (4) 2-3 example phrases showing the expected register.

findings.title: Principles are domain-relevant but terse
findings.detail: The 4 operational principles are specific to SRE (not generic platitudes), which is positive. However each is a single sentence. MVC-R and Enterprise-Scale could benefit from concrete thresholds or examples (e.g. 'minimum 99.9% availability before adding features').
findings.action: Expand each principle with 1-2 sentences of concrete guidance or a threshold example. This strengthens agent behavior consistency.

findings.title: Language inconsistency between frontmatter and body
findings.detail: Frontmatter description is in English ('Principal/Specialist Azure SRE Executor...') while the SKILL.md body is entirely in Portuguese. Section headings are Portuguese ('Princípios Operacionais', 'Ativação & Memória'). This creates a mixed-language experience.
findings.action: Choose one language consistently. If Portuguese is intentional for the target audience, translate the frontmatter description. If English is preferred, translate the body.

findings.title: No headless/autonomous mode support
findings.detail: The agent defines no headless execution mode. For an SRE executor agent, headless support could enable automated infrastructure audits, drift detection, or scheduled reliability checks without user interaction.
findings.action: Consider adding a headless mode section defining autonomous capabilities (e.g. 'audit infrastructure against WAF pillars', 'generate drift report') with input/output contracts.

summary.assessment: Agent has strong domain expertise with well-structured reference files covering all 6 Azure SRE domains (reliability, IaC/CI-CD, observability, FinOps/governance, project ledger, standard output). The identity content is effective and the principles are genuinely domain-specific (not generic platitudes). However, the agent uses non-standard Portuguese section headings instead of BMad conventions (Persona→Identity, Princípios→Principles, Ativação→On Activation), is entirely missing Overview and Communication Style sections, lacks a bmad-manifest.json for programmatic capability registration, and has no headless mode. The 7 high-severity findings are primarily structural naming issues and missing required sections — the underlying content quality is above average for a creation-phase agent. Primary remediation: rename sections to standard headings, add Overview + Communication Style, create manifest, and split the merged Ativação & Memória section.
